rag_rules: Place assume and guarantee clauses in component type annexes; use assign only in implementation annexes for output features or auxiliary variables.
Use only identifiers explicitly visible in the current component scope; never invent or copy names from examples.
generic_rules: Anchor contracts to concrete, visible model elements—never assume implicit AADL properties are in scope.
Structure guarantees to relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding internal subcomponent paths in type-level annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Annex blocks must be wrapped as annex agree {** ... **}; with semicolons terminating all declarations.
Constants and equations use the form const name : type = value; or eq name : type = expression;
anti_patterns: Do not embed AADL property names (e.g., Deadline, Period) as variables in AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Never place guarantee statements in implementation annexes—use assign for bindings instead.
